marketState: neutral — extreme 8-16D heat forecast colliding with soft near-term demand and above-normal storage
summary: Natural gas sits at $3.258, about $0.30 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.958 but within the $2.458–$3.458 band, leaving it slightly overvalued without being at a band extreme. The dominant tension is a split weather signal: 1-7D demand is 6.7% below the 5y average (soft this week), yet the 8-16D forecast is a standout +26.8% above 5y and above the 5y historical maximum — a rare extreme that could trigger a weather-driven short-covering squeeze if it verifies. Managed money is at only the 17th 5y percentile of net length (−84,909 net contracts), a historically crowded short that amplifies upside risk if bullish catalysts land. Offsetting these, storage at 2,759 Bcf is +153 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +25 Bcf build surprise, the weekly technical structure is bearish (50 EMA below 200, RSI 46), and seasonal reliability is very low (44 pp wide 10-90 spread), so the 3-week 69% probability-higher edge must be discounted heavily.

longTerm.rationale: Price is persistently above fair value across all 7-21D horizons while storage sits +153 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish build surprise, pointing to eventual mean-reversion toward the $2.96 fair-value midpoint. The weekly technical structure is bearish (50 EMA < 200, RSI 46, trend bearish) and the typical 4-week seasonal move is −2.3%. Selling into any weather-driven spike toward the $3.42–$3.45 band top offers a favorable entry with risk capped at $3.62; the 17th-percentile crowded-short COT reading is the primary threat, so the stop must sit comfortably above the band.
longTerm.invalidation: Price closes above $3.62 on volume; the 8-16D heat forecast verifies and storage draws sharply below the 5y in two consecutive EIA reports; managed-money net flips above the 50th 5y percentile.

shortTerm.entry: $3.22–$3.27 (buy on any intraday dip toward previous close or session lows)

The crowded managed-money short (17th %ile net) amplifies any weather-driven upside through forced covering. Near-term (1-7D) demand softness at −6.7% vs 5y is a headwind that keeps conviction low and sizing small.
shortTerm.invalidation: The 8-16D heat forecast is revised materially lower in the next 1-2 model runs; price breaks and closes below $3.10; or near-term demand remains deeply below 5y average for more than three additional days.
